title: clianthus scandens
intro: Clianthus scandens, also known as the pea flower vine or kaka beak, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, skin conditions, and fever. In herbal preparations, the flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
